decisions.reasoning: Iron Hands threatens an OHKO with Wild Charge, and Tornadus-Therian cannot secure a KO. Switching to Flutter Mane provides an immunity to Iron Hands' potential Fake Out or Drain Punch while threatening it with STAB super-effective damage.
Flutter Mane is already fainted (0% HP), so I must switch to a healthy Pokemon. Tornadus-Therian is the safest pivot to check Iron Hands while maintaining momentum.
Tornadus-Therian is threatened with an OHKO by Iron Hands' Wild Charge. Switching to Calyrex-Ice is the safest play to avoid losing Tornadus while maintaining a favorable matchup against Iron Hands.
High Horsepower is a 2HKO and provides the most damage against Iron Hands. Since I am at 54% and Iron Hands' Life Orb Wild Charge is a 3HKO, I can safely secure the KO before they take me down.

Calyrex-Ice is at 10% HP and will be KOed by any move from Incineroar. Switching to Tornadus-Therian preserves the Calyrex-Ice for a potential late-game revenge kill or chip damage while bringing in a healthy pivot.

Tornadus-Therian is at 48% HP and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz next turn, while Hurricane is only a 3HKO. Switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop and a fire-type resistance to pivot safely while preserving Tornadus for a potential Regenerator recovery later.
Calyrex-Ice has fainted, so I must send in a new Pokemon. Tornadus-Therian is the safest pivot to scout the opponent's next move while maintaining momentum.
I outspeed Incineroar and can pivot out to gain momentum while dealing chip damage, avoiding the 2HKO from Flare Blitz by switching to a more favorable defensive matchup.

Tornadus-Therian is at 23% and will be KO'd by Flare Blitz next turn; switching to Incineroar provides an Intimidate drop and a safe pivot to preserve Tornadus for a potential Regenerator recovery later.
